text:
The visible menus include: File, Home, Insert, Page Layout, Formulas, Data, Review, View, AutoSum, and Help.  
**Spreadsheet Headers (Columns):**
The main column headers include: Functional Area Mapping, BAU or Remi, Technology Name, Technology Type, Included in DataFlow Diagram, Contract Flow, As Cell Type, Technology Owner, and various columns for data entries (like "Bank," "Systematic Identification," "Technical Name," "Function Mapping," "Transaction Mapping," and "Commodity Area Mapping").  
**Data Content (Key words and entries visible):**
The data entries cover various business and technical terms. Visible words include:  
*   **Operational Status/Scope:** BAU (Business As Usual), Remi, BAU or Remi.
*   **Technology/Process:** Technology Name, Technology Type, System of Origination (for CIS/MAS), Reconciliation, Payment processing, MAS Analytics, Service and Manage Member (SAMM).
*   **Status/Indicators:** Y (Yes), N (No).
*   **Mappings:** Functional Area Mapping, Transaction Mapping, Commodity Area Mapping.
*   **Examples of Data Entries (Rows):** Reconciliation (under Technology Name), System of Origination (for CIS/MAS) (under Technology Type), Payment (under Functional Area Mapping), MAS Analytics (under Functional Area Mapping).  
The screenshot provides a comprehensive view of a data sheet detailing the mapping between various functional, technological, and operational elements.  
The picture is a detailed screenshot of a digital "Member summary" page, specifically for a member named Chris Whiting, which was last updated on 10 Jan 2020.  
The page is structured into several sections. Under "Personal details," a profile shows the First Name (Chris), Last Name (Whiting), Date of Birth (14 Aug 1985, 38 years old), Nationality (British), Marital Status (Married/Civil Partnership), Number of Dependants (1), Gender (Male), and Preferred name (Chris).  
The "Accounts" section provides account summary details, with available services highlighted including "Internet banking," "Calls and visits to branch," and "Banking app." Below this, "Eligibility for" shows status for a "Credit card" and "Savings & ISA."  
The "Contact details" section includes fields for Telephone, Mobile phone, Email address, and Correspondence Address. The page also includes dedicated sections for "Identification details" and "Employment details."  
The navigation bar on the left includes links for "Search again," "Account," "Contact," "Documents," "Statements," and "Alerts." At the top right, there are icons and names visible, including the name "Chris Whiting." The bottom right corner features images and names of individuals: Dominic Francoci, Ellie Tew, and Anil Gaihkwad, suggesting a gallery or related profiles, while the main area displays the details for a member's summary.  
The image is a detailed table report titled "19 SMEs consulted for payment screening, underpinning reconciliation findings" (and marked "DRAFT I" in the top right corner). The table is structured with two columns: "Name" and "Role."  
The data listed includes the following names and their corresponding roles:  
*   **Ben Ashwell:** Electronic Payments Director
*   **Rachel Cardy:** Product Owner • Assisted Service
*   **Simon Clydesdale:** Delivery Manager • Payments
*   **Vaibhav Nioding:** Test Analyst
*   **Nupur Bole:** Business Designer
*   **Shipra Saxena:** Scrum Master
*   **Karen Gosling:** Product Owner • Payments CIO
*   **Vinoth Raj Seran:** Engineer E2
*   **Tom Rees:** Product Owner • Member&Data Protection Rec
*   **Mike Rivet:** FC Analytics Senior Consultant
*   **Adam Stephens:** Engineer • E2 • Member&Data Protection Rec
*   **Jessica Mary:** Product Owner • Payments
*   **Gary Bodden:** Product Owner • Payments CIO
*   **Julian Duriez:** Senior Consultant (LexusNix)
*   **HIBA ALAM:** Engineer 3 • Payments
*   **Colin Winter:** Credit Cards
*   **Nicholas Polley:** Engineer E2
*   **Ashleigh Batson:** Cards Value Stream Lead PO
*   **Craig Barnes:** FC Analytics Consultant • Financial Crime Analytics  
The image is a detailed organizational chart titled "Tentative Roadmap for Reconcilation solution."  
The roadmap is divided into three time-based phases: NOW, NEAR, and NEXT.  
**The NOW Phase (3Q 2023 - 1Q 2024): Establish the Foundation**
Under the "M-Selected Functional Area," the goals include:
*   Decline 1 Functional Capability Area
*   Decline top 5 business control and top 5 technical / data measures  
This section details a process box labeled "System Design" which includes "Tool Configuration and Custom Coding," and subsequent steps: "Execute Recon , QA, Produce Reports," culminating in "In house Tools and Custom Coding."  
Following this, the roadmap includes two preparatory blocks:
1.  "Define Recon Strategy with DA" (providing examples like: e.g. Solution Intent, Recon type, Use of technology verification).
2.  "Gather Business Requirement (Business Control, Detailed Measures and control rules, thresholds, application and data repository mapping)."  
**The NEAR Phase (2Q 2024): Prove capabilities**
Under the "Extended Scope of Functional Areas," the objectives are listed:
*   Select all Functional Areas
*   Include all business Critical Functional / operational Measures / Metric
*   Incorporate learning from MVP  
This phase shows a second "System Design" block, which includes "Tool Configuration and Custom Coding," and subsequent steps: "Execute Recon , QA, Produce Reports," leading to "Industry Standard DQ & DR tools Commissioned."  
**The NEXT Phase: Remaining Functional Areas and BAU**
This phase details the goal: "Extend system coverage from final reporting system to originating sources for all the products."
The final process is labeled "BAU Process," which represents the ongoing operations.  
Overall, the diagram outlines the transition from establishing a foundational solution (NOW), expanding capabilities using industry standard tools (NEAR), to achieving full system coverage and routine operations (BAU).  
This image is a text-based corporate report or presentation slide titled "FCTP Data Reconciliation Initiative - Current State and Key Findings."  
Under the main title, the document also includes the section headers "Current State" and "Key Facts."  
**Current State:**
The bullet points under "Current State" detail the following points:
*   IBM has been engaged on a Time and Materials contract to assess the existing technical data reconciliation process(es) for Payment Screening.
*   We have conducted 30+ meetings with 19 SMEs over multiple weeks as well as have studied client-provided documentation, including "Payment Screening Data Reconciliation BRD" (July 12, 2023).
*   The following points summarize our specific observations from discussions with SMEs regarding Data Reconciliation along with the documents we reviewed: Lack of evidence to suggest transaction movements and data screening across various systems have been monitored or reconciled as regards the CDES within Payment Screening.
*   Data Reconciliation for Payment Screening does not appear to have been implemented yet although multiple SMEs confirmed this is a relevant and active pain point for the organisation.
name: Chunk 4/6

raw_entities.name: Reconciliation solution
raw_entities.description: A phased solution roadmap designed to establish foundational data reconciliation capabilities, prove functional scope, and extend system coverage to originating sources. The offering transitions from initial tool configuration and custom coding to commissioning industry-standard tools and ultimately achieving Business As Usual (BAU) operations.
context: Overall, the diagram outlines the transition from establishing a foundational solution (NOW), expanding capabilities using industry standard tools (NEAR), to achieving full system coverage and routine operations (BAU).

raw_entities.name: FCTP Data Reconciliation Initiative
raw_entities.description: A corporate transformation programme focused on assessing and improving the client's existing technical data reconciliation processes, specifically for payment screening. The initiative involves extensive stakeholder engagement, documentation review, and roadmap development to address current operational gaps.
context: FCTP Data Reconciliation Initiative - Current State and Key Findings.
IBM has been engaged on a Time and Materials contract to assess the existing technical data reconciliation process(es) for Payment Screening.

raw_entities.name: BAU Process
raw_entities.description: The final operational phase of the reconciliation roadmap, representing the transition to ongoing, routine system operations after foundational capabilities and extended coverage have been established.
context: The final process is labeled "BAU Process," which represents the ongoing operations.
to achieving full system coverage and routine operations (BAU).

raw_entities.name: Data Reconciliation
raw_entities.description: The core technical capability being evaluated and implemented, involving the monitoring, tracking, and reconciliation of transaction movements and data screening across multiple systems to ensure data integrity and compliance.
context: Data Reconciliation for Payment Screening does not appear to have been implemented yet
Lack of evidence to suggest transaction movements and data screening across various systems have been monitored or reconciled as regards the CDES within Payment Screening.
